name: Jira
one_liner: Atlassian's issue tracking and project management platform with a broad Cloud REST API for issues, projects, workflows, and boards.
access_notes: Anyone can create a free Jira Cloud site (yourname.atlassian.net) with no payment, then generate an API token at id.atlassian.com and immediately call the REST API via Basic auth (email:token). OAuth 2.0 (3LO) apps are self-registered in the Atlassian developer console with no approval gate. The REST API is available on the free plan with no API-specific paywall.
api_notes: Jira Cloud platform REST API v3 covers 100+ operations across issues, projects, workflows, fields, comments, attachments, JQL search, users, and permissions, plus a separate Jira Software Cloud REST API for boards/sprints. Also offers Forge and Connect app frameworks. Self-hosted Data Center has its own REST API.
mcp_notes: Atlassian ships an official remote MCP server (Rovo MCP Server) hosted at https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp connecting Jira, Confluence, JSM, Bitbucket, and Compass to AI tools via OAuth 2.1 or API tokens. Repo: github.com/atlassian/atlassian-mcp-server.
main_blocker: None significant; free self-serve credentials and a broad public REST API. Minor: API tokens now expire (default 1 year) so long-lived integrations should use OAuth.

evidence.claim: Jira Cloud REST API v3 recommends OAuth 2.0 (3LO) for non-Forge/Connect integrations; also supports Basic auth with API token
Basic auth uses base64-encoded email:api_token; API tokens managed at id.atlassian.com
Jira Cloud supports webhooks registered via admin UI or POST /rest/api/3/webhook
Atlassian ships an official remote MCP server for Jira via OAuth 2.1 or API tokens
REST API is available to free plan users with no tier-specific restrictions

open_questions: Exact API rate limits differ by plan/product; Data Center vs Cloud API differences not fully enumerated here.
